feed.title: SkillsLLM
feed.subtitle: AI tooling news and guides: SkillsLLM, Desktop Commander, Model Context Protocol, FastMCP, ClaudePluginHub, MCP Servers blog, Claude Skills Hub, OpenRouter, LiteLLM (blog + releases), Glama (blog, MCP servers, release notes), LobeHub (changelog + blog), and AI Skill Market

entry.title: Merchant of Record, Explained: Paddle vs. Stripe
entry.content: Stripe processes payments; Paddle becomes the legal seller. For a solo founder selling digital products across borders, that distinction decides who handles VAT, GST, refunds, and chargebacks — you or them.

entry.title: Why Stripe Won't Let You Take Payments Yet
entry.content: You created a Stripe account, sent a payment link, and hit 'your account cannot currently make live charges.' Here's what actually activates live mode — and the shortest path from zero to a real first dollar.

entry.title: Pillar Pages and Content Clusters for AI Citation
entry.content: Digital Applied found 86% of AI citations come from sites with 5+ interlinked pieces on one topic. Yext found a bidirectionally-linked cluster gets cited 2.7x more than an isolated page. AI crawlers read internal links as a map — here's how to structure content as one knowledge node.

entry.title: Ten Schema Tags That Get You Cited by AI
entry.content: Schema markup alone does nothing — Ahrefs tested 1,885 pages and adding it with no content change produced no citation lift. But Organization and FAQPage JSON-LD on top of real content earn 2.7x more AI citations. Here's the code and the deployment checklist.

entry.title: The Reddit Playbook for Solo Founders Who Hate Marketing
entry.content: Reddit is Perplexity's #1 source at 46.7% of top citations and #1 in Google AI Overviews at 21%. One genuinely useful 30-minute answer keeps getting pulled into AI responses indefinitely. Here's the minimal-effort playbook — and why this window won't stay open.

entry.title: Why AI Engines Cite Reddit Over Your Homepage
entry.content: AI engines trust what others say about you far more than what you say about yourself. Muck Rack found 82% of AI citations come from earned media, under 6% from brand-owned pages. The real work of GEO isn't polishing your copy — it's getting mentioned elsewhere.

entry.title: Search Traffic Forked: SEO and GEO Are Different Games
entry.content: Search just split into two paths: the ranked blue-link list and the AI-generated answer that quotes a few sources nobody clicks. SEO races for rank; GEO races to be the source the AI draws from. Here's why the distinction reorders how solo founders get found.

entry.title: The Compliance Deadline That Costs $25,000 If You Miss It
entry.content: Most 'form a US LLC' guides stop at the EIN. The part they skip can cost $25,000: Form 5472, required annually from foreign-owned LLCs even at zero revenue. Plus the BOI rule that changed in 2025 and the state report you still owe.

entry.title: Getting an EIN Without a Social Security Number
entry.content: No SSN? You can still get an EIN by phone or fax — the widely-repeated claim that the IRS killed the fax channel is wrong. Here's the SS-4 prep that keeps you from getting rejected: Line 7b, Line 11, Line 15, and the Responsible Party rule.

entry.title: Wyoming vs. Delaware vs. California for a Solo LLC
entry.content: Why an LLC beats a C-Corp for most solo founders, and which state to form in. Wyoming's $60-a-year default, Delaware's investor pull, and California's $800 franchise-tax trap — plus the registered-agent requirement nobody explains until you're stuck.

entry.title: Give Your AI Agent Legs: Control It From Your Phone
entry.content: The real payoff of a personal mesh VPN: run an AI coding agent on a home machine and drive it from your phone while the task keeps running. Here's the latency math, the security details that matter, and the one hard limit to plan around.

entry.title: Access Your Home Server From Anywhere, No Open Ports
entry.content: Tailscale turns every device you own into one private network — no public IP, no firewall rules, no forwarded ports. Here's the connection model that makes it work, and the one thing that quietly leaves you stuck on relay latency.

entry.title: Build a Waitlist Page in 30 Minutes, Zero Servers
entry.content: A concrete 6-step path from empty folder to a live email-capture page — frontend, backend, and database — deployed worldwide on Cloudflare for $0, before a single line of the real product exists. The fastest way to test if anyone wants the thing.

entry.title: Deploy Global Infrastructure for Free on Cloudflare
entry.content: Cloudflare Workers and Pages replace 'rent a VPS and configure nginx' with 'push code, it runs in 335 cities.' Here's why serverless-at-the-edge is a different model, where the free tier ceiling actually sits, and how Pages and Workers are the same primitive.

entry.title: Keeping Your Free Oracle VPS From Getting Reclaimed
entry.content: Oracle's free tier has one real string attached: it reclaims idle instances. Here's what 'idle' actually means for a dev box, the keep-alive pattern that works, why community scripts are a fragile bet, and the fix that ends the problem for good.

entry.title: Oracle's Always-Free Tier: The Server Everyone Forgets
entry.content: Oracle Cloud quietly gives away a permanent 4-core, 24GB-RAM ARM server that would cost $80-100/month on AWS. Here's why signups get rejected, the region choice you can't undo, and how to actually get the machine.

entry.title: Your First US Bank Card Without Setting Foot in America
entry.content: The four-piece kit a non-resident founder needs before a US bank says yes: ITIN, a CMRA-clean address, a US SIM, and a Wise account, plus the card application.

entry.title: The ITIN Path: A US Tax ID Without a US Address
entry.content: What an ITIN is, why a Certifying Acceptance Agent beats mailing your passport to the IRS, the 8-12 week timeline, and why it's the first US-entity domino.

entry.title: Ship a Static Site With Nothing but Git Push
entry.content: Why GitHub Pages beats Vercel, Netlify, and a VPS for a solo founder's first landing page: zero cost, no third-party login, and the deploy is a git push.

entry.title: Domain Name to Live DNS in Twenty Minutes
entry.content: Registrar tradeoffs, why to route DNS through Cloudflare, the DNSSEC gotcha that takes domains offline, and the orange-vs-grey cloud rule for GitHub Pages.

entry.title: The Questions Every Teacher Asks Before Signing Up
entry.content: Eligibility, verification, cost, data privacy, and what happens if you change schools — the practical FAQ for ChatGPT for Teachers, and a look back at the series.

entry.title: What Happens When the Free Period Ends in 2027
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers is free only through June 2027. OpenAI hasn't said what comes next. Here's what's known, what's speculation, and what to plan for regardless.

entry.title: No, Students Don't Get Free ChatGPT Too
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers is a teacher-and-staff offer, not a student one — and several big districts ban student ChatGPT outright. Untangling the confusion.

entry.title: Choosing a Pilot: A Decision Guide for Districts
entry.content: ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Copilot for your district AI pilot? A practical framework built on the stack you already run, real per-seat prices, and priorities.

entry.title: Building the Skill a Free Chatbot Can't Replace
entry.content: A free, general teacher assistant is genuinely useful — and structurally incapable of three things a purpose-built skill does. Here's the line between them.

entry.title: Where AI Tutoring Skills Fit Between the Giants
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers and Claude for Teachers are both teacher-facing productivity tools. The student-facing Socratic tutor is a niche neither giant is actually filling.

entry.title: What Claude Skill Builders Can Learn From OpenAI
entry.content: OpenAI's education playbook — narrow vertical, distribution through a trusted intermediary, free to build the habit — is a GTM lesson for anyone building a Claude Code skill.

entry.title: The Squeeze on Third-Party Classroom AI Tools
entry.content: Paid lesson-planning and differentiation tools now compete with something free from the model vendor itself. What the squeeze looks like, and which tools escape it.

entry.title: What a Free Vertical AI Product Means for EdTech
entry.content: When the model vendor ships a free, polished, education-specific product directly to teachers, the ecosystem built on its general-purpose API has to rethink what it's for.

entry.title: Piloting ChatGPT for Teachers District-Wide
entry.content: A free tool still costs you a rollout. How to pilot ChatGPT for Teachers across a district — cohort selection, a real feedback loop, and the scale-or-stop decision.

entry.title: Role-Based Access Control for School Workspaces
entry.content: Who should be an admin, who should be a member, and the RBAC misconfigurations that quietly hand a whole district's AI workspace to the wrong people. A practical guide for K-12 IT.

entry.title: Reading the Student Data Privacy Agreement
entry.content: OpenAI names itself a FERPA 'School Official' and says student data stays under the school's control. Here's what those terms actually commit — and what a privacy officer should still ask.

entry.title: SSO, MFA, and the District IT Rollout Plan
entry.content: The mechanics of a managed ChatGPT for Teachers deployment: claim the domain, wire up SAML SSO, enforce MFA, and onboard teachers in phases without flooding the help desk.

entry.title: A Procurement Checklist for ChatGPT for Teachers
entry.content: It's free, so districts skip procurement. That's the mistake. Here's the checklist an IT director should run before ChatGPT for Teachers reaches a single classroom.

entry.title: When Voice Mode Beats Typing in the Classroom
entry.content: Voice mode isn't a gimmick for teachers. It wins in the exact moments a classroom leaves your hands full and your keyboard out of reach. Here's when to talk instead of type.

entry.title: Image Generation for Worksheets and Slides
entry.content: How to use ChatGPT for Teachers' image generation for worksheets, slides, and classroom visuals — and why every AI-generated diagram needs an accuracy check first.

entry.title: Differentiation and IEP Support, Carefully Done
entry.content: How to use ChatGPT for Teachers to draft differentiated materials and IEP-aligned supports — and exactly where FERPA caution around student-identifying data has to stop you.

entry.title: Writing Parent Emails Without the Dread
entry.content: How ChatGPT for Teachers helps draft difficult parent emails — setting tone, structuring hard news, and translating — with the rule to always personalize and review first.

entry.title: Turning File Uploads Into Faster Grading Prep
entry.content: How file uploads in ChatGPT for Teachers speed up rubric-building and feedback drafting — without letting AI grade for you or auto-send anything to students.

entry.title: Co-Planning Lessons in Shared Projects
entry.content: How ChatGPT for Teachers' shared projects turn department co-planning from a Friday scramble into a durable, shared workspace. A realistic PLC workflow.

entry.title: Building a Custom GPT for Your Department
entry.content: A custom GPT turns a good prompt into a reusable tool your whole department shares. Here's how to build a rubric-grader or differentiation GPT that actually gets used.

entry.title: The Four-Way AI Vendor Race for the Classroom
entry.content: OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Microsoft are all racing for the same classrooms — with four different strategies. Here's how the board actually looks.

entry.title: Why OpenAI Chose Free Over a Freemium Tier
entry.content: OpenAI could have shipped a discounted teacher tier. It shipped a fully free one through June 2027. The go-to-market logic behind that choice is worth reading.

entry.title: Context Windows, Compared for Classroom Use
entry.content: Token context sounds abstract until you try uploading a whole class set of essays. Here's what 400K vs 1M–2M tokens actually means for a teacher's day.

entry.title: The Real Price of "Free": A Cost Comparison
entry.content: ChatGPT for Teachers is free per seat — but adoption isn't. Here's the true cost of rolling it out versus paid Gemini, Copilot, and Claude for schools.

entry.title: ChatGPT for Teachers vs Microsoft Copilot in Class
entry.content: Same GPT-5.1 engine, different chassis. A free standalone workspace versus an Office-native paid tier — and why the model isn't what decides this one.

entry.title: Gemini for Education: The Google Gravity Case
entry.content: For a Google-shop district, the AI question isn't which model is smarter. It's whether Workspace gravity and NotebookLM beat a free standalone ChatGPT.

entry.title: Claude for Education: The Other Free Offer
entry.content: OpenAI isn't the only company giving teachers AI for free. Anthropic's education play — a free K-12 offer plus a paid, partner-rich tier — is the quieter alternative.

entry.title: The Conflict-of-Interest Question in AI Training
entry.content: AI vendors are funding the union that trains teachers to use their tools. That's a real conflict worth naming — and the safeguards deserve a fair hearing too.

entry.title: Free for Teachers, Banned for Students: The Split
entry.content: OpenAI is courting teachers with a free workspace while districts ban ChatGPT for students. Is that a contradiction — or two different problems wearing one name?

entry.title: Oregon Teachers Are Calling AI Deals a "Grift"
entry.content: An Oregon NEA rank-and-file caucus wants an end to what it calls 'AI grift.' Here's what that grassroots pushback represents — and what it doesn't, within a mixed union landscape.

entry.title: What the MIT Cognitive Engagement Study Found
entry.content: An MIT study found students who used ChatGPT to write essays showed weaker cognitive engagement. Here's what that implies for student use — and why teacher use is a different case.

entry.title: Baltimore, LA, NYC, Seattle: Why They Banned ChatGPT
entry.content: Major districts banned ChatGPT over integrity and quality fears. Now OpenAI is courting those same districts with free teacher accounts. Are the two positions contradictory?

entry.title: Why Detection Tools Miss 1 in 5 AI Essays
entry.content: AI-writing detectors run roughly 80-90% accurate — and most don't publish rates at all. Here's why that's not good enough to be a teacher's primary defense.

entry.title: The Cheating Numbers Behind ChatGPT for Teachers
entry.content: A Study.com survey found over a quarter of teachers have caught a student cheating with ChatGPT. Here's what that number actually measures — and what it doesn't.

entry.title: Can a Course Make 400,000 Teachers AI-Literate
entry.content: The AFT's academy promises to train 400,000 teachers on AI in five years. But scale and depth pull against each other — can standardized training build real literacy?

entry.title: ChatGPT Foundations for Teachers, Reviewed
entry.content: A fair critical review of OpenAI's free teacher course — what it teaches well, and the gaps around AI grading, hallucination, and detection it glosses over.

entry.title: Inside the AI Skills Jam for K-12 Educators
entry.content: OpenAI's AI Skills Jam took the teacher training on the road — in-person workshops across US cities that drew 1,600+ educators. Here's what the format looks like.

entry.title: What OpenAI's Free Teacher AI Course Covers
entry.content: OpenAI Academy runs two free courses for K-12 teachers: AI Foundations and ChatGPT Foundations for Teachers. Here's the actual syllabus, section by section.

entry.title: Three Rivals, One Classroom: AFT's AI Coalition
entry.content: OpenAI, Microsoft, and Anthropic compete everywhere else — but co-funded the same $23M union program to train 400,000 teachers. Here's why.

entry.title: July Townhall: Product + Roadmap Updates
entry.content: Join the LiteLLM July townhall on Thursday, 23 July at 7:30 AM PT to learn about LiteLLM's product updates and roadmap.

entry.title: reuse-before-generate
entry.content: reuse-before-generate

entry.title: lorist
entry.content: Markdown rule "angles" plus the lorist CLI that judges a project against them, and a skill for authoring new rules.

entry.title: my-command
entry.content: Your Wish is My Command — workflow commands for shipping changes and keeping long Claude Code sessions focused.

entry.title: webview-test
entry.content: Android WebView automation & verification for Claude Code. CDP-over-ADB, declarative webview_flow (click/type/waitFor/inspect/osTap/scroll/osSwipe/osKey/goto), transient(appearsThenGone) & network-completion waits, single-call evaluate chaining.

entry.title: r2g-skills
entry.content: RTL-to-GDS-to-Graph skill collection. eda-install detects the machine and installs + verifies the open-source EDA toolchain (with a no-sudo conda path). signoff-loop drives an open-source EDA flow (Yosys + OpenROAD-flow-scripts + KLayout + Magic + Netgen + OpenRCX) from a spec or RTL to signed-off GDSII (DRC, LVS, RCX) with a self-improving fix/learn loop. def-graph converts the clean, signed-off physical design (DEF/LEF/SPEF) into PyTorch-Geometric graph datasets — five graph views (b–f), tech-lib/LEF parsing, and feature/label extraction. rtl-acquire discovers/screens RTL at corpus scale and expands it (synth-only) into pre-layout netlist graphs with dedup, quality scoring, and publish gating.

entry.title: seerxo
entry.content: Generate, audit, optimize, and research Etsy listings with Seerxo in Claude Code.

entry.title: akcli
entry.content: AI-native schematic design agent, purpose-built for KiCad (zero-dependency pure-stdlib Python CLI): author and edit .kicad_sch from JSON op-lists with net-diff safety rails, run ERC/design/intent/BOM checks, simulate on KiCad's bundled ngspice, source real parts and fetch datasheets, and import Altium .SchDoc/.SchLib/.PcbDoc read-only into the KiCad flow.

entry.title: lore
entry.content: Share Claude Code and Cowork sessions to Lore and read threads back.

entry.title: globish
entry.content: Write every document in Globish — Jean-Paul Nerrière's ~1,500-word simplified international English — with plain-writing style rules, a bundled compliance checker, and an automatic check on every file write.

entry.title: noisy-coding
entry.content: Voice coding for Claude Code, Jarvis-style — talk to your agent while it works. Ships the voice hooks and connects to the noisy-coding daemon (Docker or native).

entry.title: diffdeck
entry.content: Launch the diffdeck local diff viewer in the browser to show code changes visually to the human.

entry.title: modesofthinking
entry.content: Context-efficient multi-perspective reasoning with bounded agent councils.

entry.title: kamaal-super-mind
entry.content: Repository-agnostic guidance for implementing, testing, reviewing, and delivering reliable software changes.

entry.title: claudecad
entry.content: Verification-first parametric CAD in Claude Code: the /cad design loop (build -> verify -> render -> export STEP for Plasticity and friends).

entry.title: claude-kit
entry.content: Shareable Claude Code agent kit: orchestration, batch-dispatch, review, ADR-authoring, and memory/skill-hygiene skills plus critic/implementer/code-reviewer agents. PR-workflow hooks ship separately as claude-kit-hooks.

entry.title: kntnt-code-skills
entry.content: Kntnt's growing toolbox of code skills: a coding standard (PHP, JavaScript, TypeScript, WordPress, Gutenberg blocks, Python, Bash, and any framework added later) applied by `coder` and materialised into projects by `/coding-standard`, a project initialiser (/init) and reconciler (/doctor), a release workflow (/release, /push, /commit), and an away-from-keyboard issue-to-code orchestrator (/orchestrate).

entry.title: semctx
entry.content: Semantic code search over your repo — hybrid dense+lexical retrieval (reranked) served by the semctx engine, exposed as MCP tools (search_codebase, find_definition, find_references, file_outline, expand_chunk, ...), a codebase-retrieval skill, a prompt-context hook that injects likely-relevant code per prompt, and a PreToolUse nudge that steers built-in Grep/Glob/find toward the semctx tools in indexed repos.

entry.title: productfeeling
entry.content: ProductFeeling skill for AI agents — emotion-aware product design with /productfeeling commands.

entry.title: taskTools
entry.content: Task-list management skills backed by tasks.json/completedTasks.json: /create-task, /pick-a-task, /tackle-tasks, /close-tasks, /update-tasks, /view-task (zero-token via UserPromptSubmit hook). Usable from any directory via --plugin-dir.

entry.title: nebu-skills
entry.content: Workflow skills and lightweight routing guidance for GitHub Copilot, VS Code, and Claude-compatible agents.

entry.title: vaultmem
entry.content: Agent memory over an Obsidian vault: the session, obsidian-vault, and remember-project skills, paired with the vaultmem CLI.

entry.title: pullpush-mcp
entry.content: pullpush-mcp

entry.title: claude-recall
entry.content: Verbatim, local, cross-project search over your Claude Code memory notes (ripgrep + SQLite FTS5). No cloud, no daemon.

entry.title: aaif-events
entry.content: Skills for running AAIF (Agentic AI Foundation) in-person and online events — event content writing (announcements, Luma pages, LinkedIn carousels, recaps, speaker bios/invites, day-of slides, attendee reminders) and chapter ops (spin up a new city chapter or online event series, triage community intake, clean intake data, sync accepted organizers into the chapters list, and push/sync events to Luma with live RSVP stats).

entry.title: pr-review-loop
entry.content: PR review toolkit for Claude Code: /review-pr panel (inline) + /review-pr-watch loop, /review-pr-slack (HTML report + Slack verdict) + /review-pr-slack-watch loop with reactions as PR state. Self-improving per-repo review memory learns from developer responses across rounds; never overrides CLAUDE.md/ADRs.

entry.title: rider-skills
entry.content: JetBrains Rider agent skills for semantic refactoring, debugger-driven runtime analysis, C# test discovery, and Unreal Engine C++ authoring, testing, and live debugging.

entry.title: efio
entry.content: 反舞弊调查全流程插件市场 — 调查方法论、证据链管理、访谈分析、可视化报告、审计技术、流程分析

entry.title: agentic-senior-core
entry.content: Universal AI coding rules. Write code like a staff engineer.

entry.title: p4-code-review
entry.content: P4 Code Review (Swarm) using multiple specialized agents with confidence-based scoring, powered by the p4mcp tools

entry.title: scieng-mcp
entry.content: Science/engineering authoring tools: Mermaid/DOT/LaTeX to SVG, HTML playgrounds, OOXML edits + the scieng skill

entry.title: ship-pipeline
entry.content: A guided build pipeline that keeps senior-engineer ownership in LLM-speed development: design doc → build prompt → build review → test spec → acceptance gate → code ownership, every stage verified and documented.

entry.title: orion-protocol
entry.content: Breaks debug loops. Force Claude to investigate the runtime environment instead of pattern-matching against source code.

entry.title: design-and-build-skills
entry.content: A convention-driven agent-skill set for the full software lifecycle: Define -> Plan -> Build -> Ship. The project's Conventions are the single source of truth; the skills author the design docs and implement them as code.

entry.title: orca
entry.content: Spawn, fork, message, follow, or orchestrate Claude Code and Codex agent instances in cmux tabs.

entry.title: sway-skills
entry.content: One expert skill for this machine's sway / SwayFX 0.5.3 — window management, config, keybindings, SwayFX effects, swaybar, swaylock/swayidle, inputs, outputs, IPC, swayr, swayimg, and the live palette-theming system.

entry.title: ppt-generator
entry.content: Auto-generate presentations in Claude Code. The MCP server owns prompts, output schemas, and deterministic post-processing (validation, layout, lint, HTML/PPTX render); the client generates the slide content via a prepare/ingest handshake — no server-side LLM.

entry.title: harness-kit
entry.content: A portable Claude Code engineering harness — plan → work → verify → feedback discipline as reusable agents, skills, and hooks, plus an `introspect` skill that tailors a .claude/ setup to any repo's tech stack.

entry.title: maxforge-lab
entry.content: Agent skills for the full research-to-execution loop: researching topics, analyzing data of every kind, designing step-by-step workflows, and orchestrating tasks into verified action

entry.title: housekeeping
entry.content: Audit a GitHub repo's health — branch protection, CI, dependabot, secret scanning, lockfiles, README, license — and drive confirm-first fixes.

entry.title: skillbooks
entry.content: Every book read is a stat permanently raised. Your agent picks these up for good: mail a human would send, drafts that survive a cold reader, claims traced to their source, code swept for what a refactor left behind and for problems solved twice, and the judgment to know which decisions are truly yours.

entry.title: ceilidhboy-skills
entry.content: ceilidhboy-skills — a plugin for Claude Code

entry.title: garymike-skills
entry.content: A growing collection of practical Agent Skills: github-profile (design, build, and deploy a GitHub profile README), github-security-audit (audit and harden GitHub account and repositories), mcp-security-review (risk-rate any MCP server), secure-mcp-builder (build and harden secure MCP servers), and skill-security-review (risk-rate any agent skill on both execution surfaces).

entry.title: gavel
entry.content: The judge's hammer for test quality. Minimalism + QA discipline — one test, one verdict, move on.

entry.title: seo-kit
entry.content: Real-data SEO + GEO (LLM answer-engine citation) audit toolkit: point it at a URL for a prioritized punch list; free Tier-0 core, paid tiers opt-in.

entry.title: vs
entry.content: Plan-to-ship workflow skills for shaping ideas, building autonomously, reviewing code, and shipping PRs with agent discipline.

entry.title: ai-assisted-development
entry.content: ICEA-driven development workflow for distributed teams using Azure DevOps. Language-agnostic across .NET 8, Java/Spring Boot, Python (FastAPI/Django/Flask), Node.js, and Angular/React. Includes Dream memory system, architect skill, codebase knowledge graph (graph.json structure of record with typed edges + confidence, index+detail projection, module-wide fingerprints, git-hook staleness detection), cache-aware code review and security review, DAST dynamic scanning with OWASP ZAP, PR compliance gates, finding ledgers with dismiss workflow, sprint metrics, and product documentation.

entry.title: planner
entry.content: Persistent terminal dashboard for daily/weekly planning and Claude Code session monitoring. Tracks JIRA tasks, monitors Claude Code sessions, and surfaces permission prompts.

entry.title: trivy-plugin-vdr-skills
entry.content: Agent skills that generate the operator-attested ConfigMaps consumed by trivy-plugin-vdr for FedRAMP VDR/VER: cluster dataflow capture and compositional CR/IR/AR decision-trace attestations.

entry.title: math-mcp
entry.content: Math evaluation/CAS/stats/units tools (math-mcp server) + /math command.

entry.title: kr-research-kit
entry.content: AI-native Korean + U.S. equity research skill pack — DART filings, SEC EDGAR/XBRL, sell-side consensus, foreign-IB coverage, 5-panel charts, daily market news, and Naver-blog publishing. Primary-source-first.

entry.title: casomoltd
entry.content: Casomo shared engineering standards as a Claude Code plugin: house-standard skills, PreToolUse guard hooks, and review agents (code-review, design-xray, docs-xray)

entry.title: norma-core
entry.content: Hybrid wiki operating contract for durable LLM memory, ingest workflows, and senior-engineer execution discipline.

entry.content: Flywise MCP Server

entry.content: MCP Commerce Server

entry.content: pddikti

entry.content: uk-gazette

entry.content: openapi-anything

entry.title: SONiC MCP Server
entry.content: SONiC MCP Server

entry.title: SpaceOps Satellite Operations MCP Server
entry.content: SpaceOps Satellite Operations MCP Server

entry.title: Doc Bridge
entry.content: Doc Bridge

entry.title: xcheck
entry.content: Cross-agent audit & remediation methodology: Codex/Claude audit large projects (code or text), remediate, and cross-verify through a findings ledger with human triage gates.

entry.title: sdd-framework
entry.content: Spec-Driven Design multi-agente: orquestra features via time de agentes (Team Leader, UX/UI, Devs, QA, Security, DevOps) com modelo configurável por agente, painel de custos e aprendizado contínuo entre projetos.

entry.title: atlas
entry.content: A single-source-of-truth doc scheme that keeps a complex repo's docs from drifting (one home per fact, a per-directory shape, a drift lint). Bundles goodnight, a standalone end-of-session wrap-up task that works with or without atlas. Per-project and personal specifics live in local config, never in this package.

entry.title: distributed-testing-skills
entry.content: Two coupled skills that design and execute claim-driven, Jepsen-style test plans for distributed and stateful systems.

entry.title: x9-agent-skills
entry.content: Practical agent skills for research, browser work, delegation, instructions, and reliable workflows.

entry.title: astrolabe
entry.content: Skills for consuming the Astrolabe framework: .NET libraries (schemas, workflow, file storage, local users, search) and TypeScript/React packages (@react-typed-forms/*, @astroapps/*). Skills load on demand when Claude detects relevant work.

entry.title: claude-statusline
entry.content: Compact, color-coded status line for Claude Code — git branch, context window, 5h/weekly rate-limit windows, live session timer. Bash (zero deps) or Python.

entry.title: statusline
entry.content: Catppuccin Mocha powerline statusline for Claude Code with context tracking, git integration, and session pets — includes a setup skill covering all three variants

entry.title: batman
entry.content: Stops you wasting time. Not on bad code — on the wrong project, the feature nobody asked for, the bug you've been losing to for two hours, and the job an AI was never going to do well.

entry.title: skills
entry.content: Personal Claude Code skills — self-orchestrated task execution, Kafka/Confluent admin and dev, and more.

entry.title: retrieva
entry.content: Teach Claude Code how to create and safely edit Retrieva notes and cards.

entry.title: add-ja-subs
entry.content: 英語動画に日本語字幕を焼き込む全ローカルAIパイプライン（Whisper ASR + エージェント翻訳 + ffmpeg）。クラウドAPI・SaaS課金なし。

entry.title: endnote-for-word
entry.content: Find DOI-verified research papers, import RIS, and create native IEEE EndNote CWYW citations and bibliographies in Microsoft Word.

entry.title: go-guidelines
entry.content: Enhances code agents with Go best practices covering performance, modern syntax, generics, patterns, testing, error handling, and concurrency.

entry.title: mermail
entry.content: Privacy-first email inboxes and workspace automation for AI agents through Mermail MCP.

entry.title: signpost
entry.content: Formatting is signposting, never decoration — a readable information hierarchy for AI answers: 💡 thesis, bold conclusions, arrow-chain mechanisms, 📊 evidence, ❓→🎯 actions.

entry.title: claude-code-setup
entry.content: Mechanically-enforced verification for Claude Code: claim-guard, scope-lock, and a bash guard, wired as a plugin so your settings.json is never touched.

entry.title: acli-skills
entry.content: Claude Code skills and hooks for Jira — manage work items, sprints, and search via the Atlassian CLI (acli) without leaving your terminal

entry.title: review-loop
entry.content: Adversarial review loop: two heterogeneous external CLI reviewers (OpenAI Codex + Cursor Agent) review a plan/spec in parallel and iterate until both sign off (GO) at or above an assurance threshold.

entry.title: agentic-coding-for-humans
entry.content: Guía a colaboradores no técnicos (marketing, operaciones, finanzas, legal, o cualquier otra área sin background de desarrollo) que empiezan a usar la terminal, Claude Code y git/GitHub. Traduce cada comando u operación a lenguaje simple, y frena y explica antes de acciones irreversibles o que afectan a otros colaboradores.

entry.title: install-zapier
entry.content: Router skill that asks which Zapier surface you need (MCP, SDK, or CLI) and walks you through it.

entry.title: obsidian-md-fixer
entry.content: Fix Markdown converted from PDF/Word (by MinerU, pandoc, Marker, any converter) so tables, formulas, and images render correctly in Obsidian.

entry.title: bnr-mcp-server
entry.content: Unified B&R MCP Server — Build, Transfer, Debug, Trend for B&R Automation Studio PLCs

entry.title: argus
entry.content: argus

entry.title: HWPX Excel QGIS MCP
entry.content: HWPX Excel QGIS MCP

entry.title: sysdesign
entry.content: sysdesign

entry.title: Seamless
entry.content: Seamless

entry.title: zflow
entry.content: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents — covering the full software development lifecycle from spec to ship.

entry.title: linkout-linkedin-read
entry.content: Read LinkedIn through the user's visible local Chrome session.

entry.title: lens-temper
entry.content: LensTemper runs structured spec-driven development reviews with fresh reviewer subagents and lens-specific synthesis.

entry.title: morph
entry.content: Turn product flows into clear, audience-ready stories.

entry.title: glab-skills
entry.content: Claude Code skills and hooks for GitLab — manage MRs, issues, and CI pipelines via the glab CLI without leaving your terminal

entry.title: super-puper-powers
entry.content: SPP: a 10-phase pipeline from idea to deployed product for non-developers. Based on obra/superpowers v6.1.1.

entry.title: jira-cli
entry.content: Skills for driving jira-cli: the jira-how-to router that steers any Jira task to the CLI's embedded guides and runtime schema.

entry.title: athena
entry.content: Agent-host plugins and skills imported by Claude Code / Codex / Pi.

entry.title: ships-computer
entry.content: Facilitated development mode for Claude Code: you implement, Claude assists with specs, plans, scaffolding, and research. Be the engineer; use the AI like the Ship's Computer, not Data.

entry.title: mozie
entry.content: Find creators, run campaigns

entry.title: billing-skills
entry.content: Vendor-agnostic agent skills for subscription billing and SaaS revenue metrics: MRR waterfalls, NRR/GRR under explicit definitions, churn, dunning, proration, usage billing, and revenue recognition. Works with Stripe, Chargebee, Maxio, Zuora, Recurly, and ChargeOver data.

entry.title: dogabot
entry.content: dogabot MCP server — read-only trading tools for Pro and Institutional API keys

entry.title: claude-speckit-phx-elixir
entry.content: Spec-driven development for Elixir/Phoenix: the full spec-kit flow (constitution, specify, clarify, plan, tasks, analyze, checklist, implement, git, issues) fused with 26 Elixir specialist agents, Iron Laws enforcement hooks, and the complete Phoenix/Ecto/LiveView/Oban/Ash/OTP knowledge base. /spx:* commands.

entry.title: kztek-agents
entry.content: Bo agents/skills/hooks dieu phoi da-agent cua KZTEK dung chung cho moi project (Dispatcher, CTO, PM, BA, Tech Lead, Senior/Junior Developer, QA Lead/Engineer, DevOps Lead/Engineer, UI/UX Designer, Code Migrator, GitHub Repo Researcher, Documentation Writer, UX/UI Reviewer, Project Manager).

entry.title: claudia
entry.content: A warm, generalist companion for reflection and emotional support — evidence-informed, multi-approach, safety-floored. Not a licensed clinician, not therapy, not a medical device.

entry.title: skull
entry.content: A free, open-source Claude Code team: a leader that maps your project and assembles Sentinel plus planning, review, understanding, guardrail and security specialists. Inert markdown by default; this plugin also arms the opt-in enforcement hooks (prod-rails, guardian-test-guard, the Sentinel nudge).

entry.title: ferrox-core
entry.content: Ferrox Factory is a meta-prompting, context engineering, and spec-driven development system for AI coding agents.

entry.title: recursive-loop-audit
entry.content: Audit a named product, workflow, or codebase to find where a human is doing repeated manual diagnosis that an autonomous loop (sensor -> policy -> tool -> quality gate -> learning) could take over instead.

entry.title: content-core
entry.content: Extract text content from URLs, documents, YouTube videos, Reddit posts, and audio/video files — via CLI (uvx) or MCP.

entry.title: nats-channel
entry.content: NATS-backed channel provider for session-to-session RPC. Registers the session as a Synadia Agent Protocol for NATS v0.3 agent and stamps its A2A contextId into the plane registration.

entry.title: fable-thinking
entry.content: Reasoning protocol distilled from Claude Fable 5. Makes any model reason with evidence-grounded claims, multi-hypothesis diagnosis, concrete simulation, adversarial self-review, and calibrated outcome-first delivery.

entry.title: audition
entry.content: Ractor-readiness auditing for Ruby: check and fix gems, scripts, Rack and Rails apps with the audition gem

entry.title: customer-base-audit
entry.content: Customer-base audit framework based on Fader, Hardie & Ross — five-lens analysis of transaction data

entry.title: verona-dev-plugin
entry.content: Agent skills for Verona development from Burnt Labs.

entry.title: gipypowers
entry.content: Canonical gipyware plugin: always-on terse output (caveman) + lazy-minimal engineering (ponytail) + the superpowers skill workflow, tuned for Codex and token efficiency.

entry.title: usage-monitor
entry.content: Claude Code用量监控：各模型token用量与成本、5小时/7天限额窗口利用率与刷新时间、会话排行、工具调用统计、HTML可视化报告、状态栏实时显示

entry.title: openspec-superpowers
entry.content: Autonomous OpenSpec delivery with Superpowers execution discipline.

entry.title: crystal-knows
entry.content: DISC personality intelligence for your calls and emails. Resolve anyone's Crystal profile and analyze meeting transcripts through the lens of each participant's DISC type.

entry.title: mherzog4-skills
entry.content: Skills by mherzog4 — starting with volley, intellectual tennis for stress-testing ideas.

entry.title: maestro
entry.content: Route consult, critique, and implement work to non-Anthropic frontier models (GPT-5.6 and friends) through a local CLIProxyAPI proxy, and scaffold your own reusable flows.

entry.title: devlog
entry.content: A lightweight project-memory discipline for Claude Code: newest-first progress log, append-only decision log, file-per-issue tickets, and an evidence-summary session-wrap before every commit.

entry.title: usagemon
entry.content: Surface AI coding-tool usage quotas (Claude / Antigravity / Codex / Grok) to agents via the Usagemon CLI

entry.title: platfory
entry.content: Earn a share of ad revenue from one tasteful sponsored line shown while Claude Code thinks. Opt-in; you keep 70%. Not affiliated with or endorsed by Anthropic.

entry.title: novel-to-game
entry.content: 把小说转成游戏企划与可完整游玩的网页游戏原型。

entry.title: superpowers
entry.content: Core skills library for Claude Code: TDD, debugging, collaboration patterns, and proven techniques

entry.title: lucid
entry.content: Manually curate native Claude Code memory stores — dedup and reconcile in place (git-versioned for recovery), while holding value-contradictions for your review instead of auto-resolving them. Verbs: /lucid:curate, /lucid:resolve, /lucid:history, /lucid:restore, /lucid:setup, /lucid:schedule. Triggers on 'curate memory', 'clean up memory store', 'lucid memory'.

entry.title: notion-skills
entry.content: Use a Notion database as your personal, agent-agnostic skill store. Register skills as Notion pages (name + trigger keywords + instructions); this plugin routes matching Claude Code requests to them and keeps a local registry in sync — while Notion AI and other agents consume the same skills directly.

entry.title: buckets
entry.content: Throwaway runtime buckets for AI agents — resolve, fetch, and run any tool in an isolated environment

entry.title: ongame
entry.content: ongame — the closed-source CLI transport layer for the ongame game-making assistant. This is a static, near-permanent shim; the real, self-updating logic lives in the ongame-cli binary it launches.

entry.title: gitcad
entry.content: gitcad

entry.title: context-fabric
entry.content: context-fabric

entry.title: @oksigenia/checker-mcp
entry.content: @oksigenia/checker-mcp

entry.content: XActions MCP Server

entry.title: moji-mcp-worker
entry.content: moji-mcp-worker

entry.title: practice-fusion-mcp
entry.content: practice-fusion-mcp

entry.title: hugo-mcp-server
entry.content: hugo-mcp-server

entry.title: thinkies
entry.content: Reasoning toolkit of 48 skills — decomposition, questioning, assumption excavation, perspective shifts, decision analysis, ideation, synthesis, and epistemic integrity checks. Installed skills invoke as /thinkies:<name>.

entry.title: asp
entry.content: ASP core 強制力：SessionStart 審計（Draft-ADR→settings.local.json deny 注入）+ PreToolUse commit 閘，外加 15 個 asp-* skills 與 3 個 /asp:* commands。enforcement-first（SPEC-014）：核心 deny+閘零 .asp/ 『資源』依賴（腳本 plugin-bundled，僅讀 docs/adr、寫 settings.local.json；POC-1 證）；profile/level 內容於 dual-path 期間仍由 installer 提供。experimental/showcase 依 ADR-017 不映射、不載入（實體隨 source:'./' 一併 clone 但 inert）。

entry.title: codesign
entry.content: Give your AI agent a real design engine — generate and edit designs and export print-ready PDFs over MCP, powered by CE.SDK. Requires Node.js >= 20 on your PATH — without it the server fails silently (see README).

entry.title: software-engineering-team
entry.content: A complete, orchestrated software and product development team: analysis, planning, architecture, design system, implementation, review, verification and a containerized environment.

entry.title: vibe-ic
entry.content: AI-Native IC Design plugin for Claude Code — one install bundles and auto-registers the MCP-EDA server that wraps the open-source EDA toolchain and lab hardware. Two entry points into one Phase 1: (A) natural-language prompt/dialogue, or (B) existing design docs → layered design JSON (L-docs). Phase 2: RTL → lint → sim → formal → FPGA SOF + on-board test. Phase 3: synth → STA → DFT → PnR → DRC/LVS → GDS → tapeout. Analog A1-A9 (sizing → layout → hardmacro) and Mixed-signal M1-M4 (merge → AMS co-sim → PV) interleaved per phase. Strict canonical-flow compliance gate plus an anti-fabrication doctrine enforced as deterministic gates (provenance audit-chain, chip-AGNOSTIC source guard, real-artifact + SHA256 attestation, gate self-assertion, benchmark-path consistency) — no fabrication, no hallucinated PASS.

entry.title: project-management-office
entry.content: Project Management Office: the shared operations backbone for every team plugin. One central database for projects, epics, stories, tasks with attempt history, dependencies, DoD records, work orders, findings and audit events, written only through its CLI, recorded deterministically via hooks, and viewed through a read-only web dashboard.

entry.title: inspeximus
entry.content: Self-correcting memory for Claude Code. Facts you correct stay corrected, deletions leave a signed receipt, and storing something never calls a model.

entry.title: swift-modern-dev
entry.content: Modern Swift/iOS development toolkit — enforces @Observable, NavigationStack, Swift Concurrency patterns and blocks deprecated APIs

entry.title: pstuart-publishing
entry.content: Publishing toolkit — professional book publishing (PDF/EPUB/covers), blog authoring, and content-creation workflows

entry.title: warm-25
entry.content: Your first 25 orders are already in your network. /warm-25 reads your LinkedIn connections export, ranks every person against your offer, and builds a branded Warm 25 dashboard with heat scores, reasons, and your first 5 no-pitch messages. /daily-5 runs the 20-minute daily loop. /reply-coach tells you exactly what to send when they reply.

entry.title: jg-product-design-skills
entry.content: 产品设计方法论:从粗需求到 PRD、到高保真原型、再到禅道研发需求的完整工作流。包含 requirements2prd(七步法+需求规模定级+商业本质三问+五看三定+苏格拉底引导+历史债务子框架)、prd2prototype(七步法+回写PRD+锁定迭代+拉通设计/技术/QA)、prd2zentao(锚定第四章拆需求+确认产品+模块只到二级+API批量upsert)、proto-check(原型自查:产品设计自查表88条+七大易用原则量化标准52条,输出双自查报告+可直接粘贴的整改要求)四个 skill。

entry.title: nuxt-modern-dev
entry.content: Modern Nuxt/Vue development toolkit — enforces Tailwind-only styling, validates nginx configs, and provides accessibility and security guidance

entry.title: dev-safety-net
entry.content: Universal developer safety guardrails — blocks dangerous commands, verifies builds, provides session context, and scans repo health

entry.title: de-residency
entry.content: Conversational coach for non-EU expats preparing for German government appointments — visa, residency, citizenship. Researches every claim live and cites each factual statement with a URL.

entry.title: Psychosynth MCP
entry.content: Psychosynth MCP

entry.title: agent-skills
entry.content: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents — covering the full software development lifecycle from spec to ship.

entry.title: flutter
entry.content: Best-of-breed general Flutter engineering skills — architecture, Riverpod 3.x state, testing, persistence, i18n/RTL, accessibility, performance, plus codegen/CI/migration runbooks — usable in any Flutter app.

entry.title: hypervibe
entry.content: Bootstrap a full-stack T3 project (Next.js + tRPC + Drizzle + Tailwind + shadcn/ui) with modular addons for database, auth, payments, email, storage, i18n, analytics, domain, and SEO. Deploys to Vercel with GitHub integration.

entry.title: sixth-sense
entry.content: A fork of obra/superpowers built around explicit model and effort routing for every subagent, stack-aware conventions for Java, Angular, React, Next.js, and Python, and a two-pass edge-case reviewer (Dylan Dog) instead of a single review pass.

entry.title: lcd
entry.content: Context-economy agentic dev methodology: process weight adapts to the work (Quick / Standard / Deep lanes) and durable artifacts make a context reset cheap.

entry.title: world-clock
entry.content: Time tools for Claude from worldclock.pro: current time anywhere, timezone conversion, DST transitions, coordinate lookup, and city search. Bundles the remote MCP server plus a skill that knows how to use it.

entry.title: graph
entry.content: Graph-engineer any goal: decompose it into a DAG of specialized subagents with JSON-schema hand-offs on every edge, then execute it as a background workflow.

entry.title: andarwaly-skills
entry.content: Curated agent skill packages by Andarwa — Obsidian, Figma, productivity, and note-taking tools.

entry.title: maestro
entry.content: 에이전트 머지 승인 컨트롤 데크 — 세션 시작 시 로컬 승인 서버 자동 기동, Claude 응답 완료 시 승인 요청 전송

entry.title: gh-toolkit
entry.content: Eight GrowthHackers Claude Code skills: gh-repo-mission, gh-kb-note, gh-launch-post, gh-publish, gh-agent-org, gh-brand-voice, gh-translate-button, gh-adversarial-review. Auto-discovered from the skills/ directory.

entry.title: tdd-harness
entry.content: A mechanical RED gate for test-driven development: a PreToolUse hook blocks every write to production code until a test has been run and watched to fail. Ships the gate, cycle tracking, evidence-gated completion, TDD subagents, and a scaffolder that installs the whole thing into a fresh repo.

entry.title: gitmap-pro
entry.content: Report Claude Code agent presence and attention to your gitmap server — live fleet view plus a durable log of which files your agents read, search, and edit.

entry.title: ai-doc-gen
entry.content: Multi-agent codebase analysis and documentation generation: parallel analyzers for structure, dependencies, data flow, request flow, and APIs, plus README and AI-rules (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, Cursor rules) generators.

entry.title: lawrencium-hpc
entry.content: Answer Lawrencium (LRC) & Einsteinium HPC questions from the scienceit-docs Markdown source instead of scraping HTML from scienceit-docs.lbl.gov.

entry.title: cc-notify
entry.content: Hear when Claude Code is done. A melody that encodes the outcome, then a spoken one-line summary — including the question when Claude stops to ask you something.

entry.title: grok-build-guardian
entry.content: Makes coding agents finish work cleanly: blocks catastrophic shell commands before they run, and won't let the agent end its turn until formatting, type-checks, and tests pass. Local, auditable, no network.

entry.title: wade-skills
entry.content: Agent skills Wade Foster (Co-Founder & CEO, Zapier) uses day-to-day — War Council, Meeting Follow-Up Pipeline, and Exec Weekly Agenda Generator.

entry.title: forkscope
entry.content: Local-first risk simulation for EVM & Solana. Drive the Forkscope CLI from Claude Code: reconstruct a dependency graph, run forked-mainnet scenarios, and summarize risk before a PR — all on your machine.

entry.title: claude-harness-visualizer
entry.content: Visualize your Claude Code harness — agents by model tier, hooks across the session lifecycle, orchestrator routing, and the review pipeline — as an interactive page

entry.title: harness-factory
entry.content: Scaffold a language-specific agent-development harness — AGENTS.md constitution, AST-level boundary linter, CI gate, and spec-as-contract issue templates — into any repo, as a native Claude Code skill.

entry.title: domain-check
entry.content: Cross-platform, dependency-free domain availability checker. Five-source consensus (RDAP, WHOIS, authoritative DNS, A/AAAA, TLS), GDPR-aware for .de/.eu/.at/.ch/.es, single + bulk + naming co-pilot. Pure Python 3.11+ stdlib.

entry.title: reelier
entry.content: Record an agent's tool-call workflow once, replay it deterministically at 0 tokens, and diff runs to catch drift — CI + snapshot tests for MCP tool-call workflows.

entry.title: dsg-governance
entry.content: AI Governance Control Plane that makes every Agent decision provable, auditable, and replayable — Deterministic Safety Gate (Z3/SMT), Cryptographic Proof, Replayable Governance, Compliance Evidence Pack, and Hermes Controlled Executor. Built for VC, CTO, Enterprise Buyer, and Governance Team standards.

entry.title: react-performance
entry.content: React performance optimization agent for identifying and resolving bottlenecks

entry.title: dotnet-advanced
entry.content: Design the architecture and runtime composition of modern .NET applications.

entry.title: example-skills
entry.content: Collection of example skills demonstrating various capabilities including skill creation, MCP building, visual design, algorithmic art, internal communications, web testing, artifact building, Slack GIFs, and theme styling

entry.title: hcb-dev
entry.content: Personal developer baseline: skills for resolving dependency versions from the registry (keeping GitHub Dependabot config in sync), pulling current library docs from Context7, and driving a GitHub pull request from a finished branch to a merged PR.

entry.title: csharp
entry.content: C# code style for coding agents (Claude Code, Codex CLI): modern C# conventions under Nullable and TreatWarningsAsErrors, loaded before writing or editing C#.

entry.title: justfile
entry.content: Analyzes project structure to recommend high-value justfile recipes

entry.title: markdown-docs
entry.content: MCP server for efficient navigation of large markdown documents (datasheets, standards, manuals).

entry.title: address-pr-feedback
entry.content: Fetch, evaluate, and resolve unaddressed PR review comments automatically

entry.title: blackraptor-dev-team
entry.content: A governed AI development team: a process orchestrator, a principal architect, engineers, and blocking-gate reviewers (security, privacy, compliance, red-team, and the evidence-auditor research gate). Ships with shared skills — excellence-pass, research-integrity, gate-verdict-format, enforcement-liveness, change-record, stride-review, owasp-llm-checklist, expand-contract-migration.

entry.title: perfectlittlebusiness
entry.content: The Perfect Little Business plugin. Your AI Operating System (AIOS), installed in one move. It connects you to your Authority HQ portal, builds your AIOS Brain through five foundational tools (Avatar, Offer, Conviction, Brand, and Source of Truth) that run live from Authority HQ, sets up your file structure, stands up your Cowork OS dashboard, and installs the operating skills that keep the system self-improving: /start and /end for every session, /learn and /decide to capture what you discover, /hygiene to keep it healthy, and /how-to for anything else.

entry.title: 1c-odata
entry.content: MCP server for querying 1C:Enterprise databases over OData V3 — live schema introspection and read-only queries.

entry.title: revit-solution
entry.content: Build and maintain Revit add-in projects with Nice3point.Revit.Sdk and Nice3point.Revit.Templates.

entry.title: vietle-mobile
entry.content: Skills cho app React Native (app-host, app-guest): build màn hình, feature nhiều màn, port màn, UX mobile.

entry.title: MCP Server Control

entry.title: redtail-mcp

entry.title: compass-mcp
entry.content: compass-mcp

entry.title: fpv-sim-mcp
entry.content: fpv-sim-mcp

entry.title: claude_bottombar
entry.content: A Claude Code status bar showing project directory, git branch, token usage, MCP servers, and active LSP servers

entry.title: the-musketeer
entry.content: Grok web UI automation through agent-browser

entry.title: write-commit
entry.content: コミットメッセージと PR 本文の共通規約(プレフィックス語彙・件名の粒度・本文の骨組み)。

entry.title: dev-tool-bootstrap
entry.content: 運用ツールを一撃でスキャフォールドする初期化スキル集: 自前エラートラッカー (mini-sentry) 導入 / READ-only admin ダッシュボード導入

entry.title: dev-flow
entry.content: 跨語言開發工作流 — git 生命週期、工作編排、context 治理。Conventional Commits / Git Flow / worktree，收斂多專案共通做法。

entry.title: analytics-tools
entry.content: プロダクト分析ツール導入スキル: Amplitude を Web / モバイル / サーバの 3 面に導入 (イベント設計・命名規約・検証フローまで)

entry.title: llm-eval
entry.content: LLM エージェント/生成機能の eval 基盤を、手法非依存の基盤（ディレクトリ構造・冪等刻印 / gold 構築 / モデル・プロンプト注入 seam）と、拡張可能な評価/最適化手法（code-based 照合 / LLM-judge BINEVAL / GEPA プロンプト最適化）に分けて敷設するスキル群。手法が増えても同じ evals/ 構造・run report スキーマ・CLI 契約を共有して破綻しない設計。

entry.title: designers
entry.content: مهارات التصميم: واجهات UI/UX احترافية، الهوية البصرية، تصميم الواجهات الأمامية، الحركات والانتقالات، والـ web artifacts.

entry.title: logscope
entry.content: Timestamped serial-log monitoring with inline agent annotations, a web UI, and a durable per-project knowledge file. Lets Claude observe device logs, send commands, and run external steps without logs being pasted into chat.

entry.title: ok-planner
entry.content: The ok-planner documentation corpus — concepts, provable stories and decisions, an issue queue — plus the sprint planning ceremony and corpus verbs (audit, prove)

entry.title: enkaku
entry.content: Enkaku RPC discovery and domain skills: protocol, client, server, and transports.

entry.title: setup-completo
entry.content: Orquestador: instala el setup estándar completo (skill inicializar-custom) — preferencias, memoria, planes y commits de una pasada.

entry.title: swetrix-events
entry.content: Track pageviews, custom events, heartbeats, errors and revenue in Swetrix via MCP.

entry.title: lightspeed-labs
entry.content: Lightspeed Labs platform integration: publish prototypes, collect feedback, run usability tests, synthesize results, and generate research reports.

entry.title: review-pr
entry.content: プロジェクト横断のコードレビュー手順と出力フォーマットの骨格。具体的な観点は各リポジトリの .claude/skills 側に置く。

entry.title: social-media
entry.content: مهارات السوشال ميديا: كتابة المنشورات، الهوكات، سكربتات الريلز، الكاروسيل، تحسين الحسابات، وتصميم الثامبنيلات.

entry.title: add-skill
entry.content: Scaffold a new skill in the lightspeed-labs-skills marketplace. Use when the user wants to add a skill to the marketplace, scaffold a new marketplace plugin, or says 'new marketplace skill' or 'add a skill'. Do NOT trigger for personal skill creation.

entry.title: small-business
entry.content: مهارات إدارة الأعمال الصغيرة: التدفق النقدي، الفواتير، الحملات، متابعة العملاء، الرواتب، والتقارير الدورية.

entry.title: planificar
entry.content: Skill de análisis: interroga un plan contra la sabiduría del repo (glosario + decisiones + conocimiento) hasta llegar a un acuerdo, y lo critica (problemas, faltantes, oportunidades, sobreingeniería). Reemplaza grill-with-docs. Operacional, no se instala por-repo.

entry.title: xbrd-gdsp-fknpft
entry.content: Multimodel dispatch orchestration and benchmark workflows

entry.title: the-almanacker
entry.content: NotebookLM web UI automation through agent-browser

entry.title: fastify
entry.content: Fastify + pino サーバ向けスキル: dev 環境で 4xx / 5xx の原因を 1 行ログで追えるようにする基盤とルート側規約

entry.title: developers
entry.content: مهارات المطورين: بناء المشاريع، MCP، الاختبار، الذاكرة، تخطيط وتنفيذ المهام البرمجية، ومهارات Superpowers للتطوير المنهجي.

entry.title: legal
entry.content: مهارات القسم القانوني: مراجعة العقود، فرز NDA، الامتثال، المذكرات القانونية، ومراجعة الموردين.

entry.title: ok-plumbline
entry.content: Code standards for agentic maintenance — ships the Plumbline methodology: rules, lint binary, PostToolUse hook, and compliance skills

entry.title: basic
entry.content: 汎用スキル: 表現・デザイン思考 / セッション振り返り (retro) / スケジュール提案の抑制 / 意味のある単位でのコミット分割

entry.title: swetrix-statistics
entry.content: Read-only Swetrix analytics queries for traffic, performance, errors, funnels and goals via MCP.

entry.title: qcom-skills-contribute
entry.content: Turn local edits to an installed skill from this catalog into a DCO-signed topic-branch commit and, on request, the upstream pull request

entry.title: ok-workspaces
entry.content: Workspace hygiene for parallel agent work — worktree-per-job, isolated runtime stacks, content-addressed artifacts — tailored to the project's declared stack profile

entry.title: hyva-utilities
entry.content: Supporting utility skills mainly invoked by other Hyva skills: Tailwind CSS compilation, shell command execution, theme listing, and CMS component dumps.

entry.title: create-pr
entry.content: PR 作成の作業フロー(ベース判定 → 差分集約 → テンプレ検出 → 作成)。タイトル・本文の文面規約は write-commit に委譲する。

entry.title: skill-catalog
entry.content: Extra skills extending Claude/Copilot/Codex base capabilities

entry.title: the-puppeteer
entry.content: Web UI task execution and long-running assistant flows

entry.title: desktop-avalonia
entry.content: Avalonia 桌面 .NET 能力 — MVVM 慣例、跨平台路徑處理、桌面應用建置與發佈。

entry.title: database-sqlserver
entry.content: SQL Server 資料庫能力 — table/view 撰寫慣例、TableDescription 文件生成、EF Core migration 工具與疑難排解。

entry.title: finance
entry.content: مهارات المالية والمحاسبة: القيود، التسويات، القوائم المالية، تحليل الفروقات، الإقفال الشهري، ودعم التدقيق.

entry.title: create-skill
entry.content: Agent Skills 標準形式で新しいスキルを作成する手順・命名/配置ルール・段階的読み込みのガイド。

entry.title: ui-ux
entry.content: UI/UX 設計知識庫 — 風格系統、版面配置、互動慣例、資訊呈現，供介面設計與審查取用。

entry.title: DPIA-MCP
entry.content: DPIA-MCP

entry.title: mcp-sql-query
entry.content: mcp-sql-query

entry.title: MCP Agent Trace
entry.content: MCP Agent Trace

entry.title: PatSnap Patent & Literature Search
entry.content: PatSnap Patent & Literature Search

entry.title: csv-analyzer-mcp
entry.content: csv-analyzer-mcp

entry.title: Türkiye Vergi MCP
entry.content: Türkiye Vergi MCP

entry.title: marketing
entry.content: 45+ مهارة تسويقية: الإعلانات، كتابة المحتوى، SEO، البريد، التسعير، إطلاق المنتجات، وعلم نفس التسويق.

entry.title: frontend-vue
entry.content: Vue 3 + TypeScript + Quasar 前端能力 — 架構分層、composable 抽取、Chrome DevTools 除錯、i18n、OpenSpec 工作流，含 code-reviewer/test-engineer agents。

entry.title: lightspeed-prototype
entry.content: Build a branded, front-end-only Lightspeed internal-tool prototype from a plain-English idea using the real Helios design system, and export it as a Labs test bundle (index.html + labs-test.json). Offers ISPM starter scenarios or turns any input-to-output tool idea into a realistic prototype. The standard workshop prototype path.

entry.title: backend-dotnet
entry.content: .NET Clean Architecture 後端能力 — CQRS / Repository / EF Core / Dapper 產碼、單元測試、TDD，含 architect/developer/reviewer/tester agents。

entry.title: update-skill
entry.content: Update or improve an existing skill in the lightspeed-labs-skills marketplace. Use when the user wants to update a marketplace skill, bump a plugin version, or says 'update marketplace skill' or 'update a skill'. Do NOT trigger for edits to personal skills.

entry.title: railway
entry.content: Railway へのデプロイ・運用スキル: セットアップの落とし穴 (GitHub 接続 / railway.json / Railpack / region / monorepo) と、稼働 service のログからのエラー調査・レポート作成

entry.title: secure-npm-package
entry.content: Set up a secure npm package release process — Trusted Publishing with staged publishes from GitHub Actions — and walk the user through the required npmjs.com and GitHub settings

entry.title: ok-doctor
entry.content: Suite upkeep dispatcher — discovers which ok-plugins a project integrates and drives each one's own doctor/affirm verbs; knows the integration contract, never plugin internals

entry.title: spoderman
entry.content: Attack harness workflows and hook safety research

entry.title: herramientas
entry.content: Gestión de Herramientas del repo: las tools del Propósito (script/skill local/MCP local) en .claude/herramientas/ con registro INDICE.md (columna Tipo) + lint. Los lints de subsistema no son herramientas. Depende de memoria-local.

entry.title: swetrix-admin
entry.content: Manage Swetrix projects, funnels, annotations, views and organisations via MCP.

entry.title: design
entry.content: UI/UX 設計・ユーザビリティ評価系スキル

entry.title: Benchmarking the LiteLLM Rust AI Gateway: Overhead, Memory, and Cost
entry.content: AIGatewayBench measures the overhead an AI gateway adds on top of the upstream model, isolated against a deterministic mock, across LiteLLM (Rust), LiteLLM (Python v1), Portkey, and Bifrost. The LiteLLM Rust gateway has the lowest overhead and memory footprint of the four.

entry.title: asc
entry.content: Production workflows for shipping apps with the unofficial App Store Connect CLI, including builds, TestFlight, metadata, submissions, signing, screenshots, and Apple Ads.

entry.title: dlang-skills
entry.content: A collection of D programming language expert skills covering syntax and semantics, Phobos, druntime, templates and metaprogramming, ranges, concurrency, tooling, and language evolution.

entry.title: izakfilmalter-skills
entry.content: Composable bug, feature, and Slack workflows that finish with ready-for-review GitHub pull requests.

entry.title: college-launch
entry.content: An operations and funding copilot for a student's first year of college. Runs an onboarding questionnaire, ingests documents (aid letters, bills, benefit certificates, degree audits, schedules), allocates any mix of aid across each term, tracks deadlines and risks, and keeps a structured knowledge base in whatever tool you use.

entry.title: dialogue
entry.content: Agent Skills that discover a shared Knowledge Repository via .dialogue.yml and keep a single source of truth for decisions. dialogue-knowledge manages current state; dialogue-history reconciles divergent Change Records (Merge) and Packs closed history. Conflicts are escalated to a human, and staleness and duplication are continuously reconciled.

entry.title: r0x-os
entry.content: AI agents can use the official r0x facilitator on Robinhood Chain directly from Claude Code: check balances, prices and transactions, trade any token and manage Uniswap V3 liquidity positions, check yield and bridge quotes, chat with the r0x agent, and pay for capabilities in USDG, one x402 call at a time.

entry.title: seed-protocol
entry.content: Find, vet, and charter goodness-based projects — seeds so genuinely good they grow themselves. Brainstorm from service (sow), test through four gates with a blind vetter (vet), charter for honest growth (tend). Refuses extraction; always breeds the honest twin.

entry.title: sky-cua
entry.content: Cross-platform desktop and browser computer use: native app discovery, accessibility trees, screenshots, input actions, window targeting, and Chrome-family browser automation through MCP.

entry.title: symfony-contribution-skills
entry.content: AI agent skills for contributing to Symfony: PHP coding standards, naming conventions, the backward compatibility promise, and reStructuredText documentation standards.

entry.title: claude-harness-builder
entry.content: Interactive wizard that builds a Claude Code harness in your project: settings.json permission presets, a CLAUDE.md skeleton, enforcement hooks for 7 hook events with ready-to-use script templates, and custom slash commands generated from your description. Invoke /claude-harness-builder:wizard or just ask to 'set up a harness for this project'.

entry.title: rama-marketing
entry.content: Evidence-led paid media, audience architecture, and complete customer journey strategy for Meta, Google, TikTok, and LinkedIn Ads.

entry.title: shippusharma-skills
entry.content: Shippu Sharma agent skills for real engineering and developers.

entry.title: AdvinPay MCP
entry.content: AdvinPay MCP

entry.content: jira-mcp

entry.content: linkedin-mcp-server

entry.content: mcp-expense-tracker

entry.content: mcp-lint-server
